text: Fill in the blanks about introductory embryology concepts
questions: Human prenatal development spans approximately *38/thirty-eight* weeks (266 days) from *fertilisation/fertilization* to delivery.
The first two weeks of development are called the *all-or-none/all or none* period because exposure to teratogens either kills the embryo or causes no malformation.
The term *ontogeny/ontogenesis* refers to the developmental history of an individual organism, while *phylogeny/phylogenesis* refers to the evolutionary history of a species.

The period of greatest susceptibility to teratogens is the *embryonic/embryonic period* period (weeks 3-8) because this is when *organogenesis/organ formation* occurs and all major organ systems are being established.
